690	def register_to_config(init):
691	    r"""
692	    Decorator to apply on the init of classes inheriting from [`ConfigMixin`] so that all the arguments are
693	    automatically sent to `self.register_for_config`. To ignore a specific argument accepted by the init but that
694	    shouldn't be registered in the config, use the `ignore_for_config` class variable
695	
696	    Warning: Once decorated, all private arguments (beginning with an underscore) are trashed and not sent to the init!
697	    """
698	
699	    @functools.wraps(init)
700	    def inner_init(self, *args, **kwargs):
701	        # Ignore private kwargs in the init.
702	        init_kwargs = {k: v for k, v in kwargs.items() if not k.startswith("_")}
703	        config_init_kwargs = {k: v for k, v in kwargs.items() if k.startswith("_")}
704	        if not isinstance(self, ConfigMixin):
705	            raise RuntimeError(
706	                f"`@register_for_config` was applied to {self.__class__.__name__} init method, but this class does "
707	                "not inherit from `ConfigMixin`."
708	            )
709	
710	        ignore = getattr(self, "ignore_for_config", [])
711	        # Get positional arguments aligned with kwargs
712	        new_kwargs = {}
713	        signature = inspect.signature(init)
714	        parameters = {
715	            name: p.default for i, (name, p) in enumerate(signature.parameters.items()) if i > 0 and name not in ignore
716	        }
717	        for arg, name in zip(args, parameters.keys()):
718	            new_kwargs[name] = arg
719	
720	        # Then add all kwargs
721	        new_kwargs.update(
722	            {
723	                k: init_kwargs.get(k, default)
724	                for k, default in parameters.items()
725	                if k not in ignore and k not in new_kwargs
726	            }
727	        )
728	
729	        # Take note of the parameters that were not present in the loaded config
730	        if len(set(new_kwargs.keys()) - set(init_kwargs)) > 0:
731	            new_kwargs["_use_default_values"] = list(set(new_kwargs.keys()) - set(init_kwargs))
732	
733	        new_kwargs = {**config_init_kwargs, **new_kwargs}
734	        getattr(self, "register_to_config")(**new_kwargs)
735	        init(self, *args, **init_kwargs)
736	
737	    return inner_init
738	
739

8	class DDIMSchedulerTest(SchedulerCommonTest):
9	    scheduler_classes = (DDIMScheduler,)
10	    forward_default_kwargs = (("eta", 0.0), ("num_inference_steps", 50))
11	
12	    def get_scheduler_config(self, **kwargs):
13	        config = {
14	            "num_train_timesteps": 1000,
15	            "beta_start": 0.0001,
16	            "beta_end": 0.02,
17	            "beta_schedule": "linear",
18	            "clip_sample": True,
19	        }
20	
21	        config.update(**kwargs)
22	        return config
23	
24	    def full_loop(self, **config):
25	        scheduler_class = self.scheduler_classes[0]
26	        scheduler_config = self.get_scheduler_config(**config)
27	        scheduler = scheduler_class(**scheduler_config)
28	
29	        num_inference_steps, eta = 10, 0.0
30	
31	        model = self.dummy_model()
32	        sample = self.dummy_sample_deter
33	
34	        scheduler.set_timesteps(num_inference_steps)
35	
36	        for t in scheduler.timesteps:
37	            residual = model(sample, t)
38	            sample = scheduler.step(residual, t, sample, eta).prev_sample
39	
40	        return sample
41	
42	    def test_timesteps(self):
43	        for timesteps in [100, 500, 1000]:
44	            self.check_over_configs(num_train_timesteps=timesteps)
45	
46	    def test_steps_offset(self):
47	        for steps_offset in [0, 1]:
48	            self.check_over_configs(steps_offset=steps_offset)
49	
50	        scheduler_class = self.scheduler_classes[0]
51	        scheduler_config = self.get_scheduler_config(steps_offset=1)
52	        scheduler = scheduler_class(**scheduler_config)
53	        scheduler.set_timesteps(5)
54	        assert torch.equal(scheduler.timesteps, torch.LongTensor([801, 601, 401, 201, 1]))
55	
56	    def test_betas(self):

58	            self.check_over_configs(beta_start=beta_start, beta_end=beta_end)

60	    def test_schedules(self):

102	    def test_variance(self):
103	        scheduler_class = self.scheduler_classes[0]
104	        scheduler_config = self.get_scheduler_config()
105	        scheduler = scheduler_class(**scheduler_config)
106	
107	        assert torch.sum(torch.abs(scheduler._get_variance(0, 0) - 0.0)) < 1e-5
108	        assert torch.sum(torch.abs(scheduler._get_variance(420, 400) - 0.14771)) < 1e-5
109	        assert torch.sum(torch.abs(scheduler._get_variance(980, 960) - 0.32460)) < 1e-5
110	        assert torch.sum(torch.abs(scheduler._get_variance(0, 0) - 0.0)) < 1e-5
111	        assert torch.sum(torch.abs(scheduler._get_variance(487, 486) - 0.00979)) < 1e-5
112	        assert torch.sum(torch.abs(scheduler._get_variance(999, 998) - 0.02)) < 1e-5

130	        assert abs(result_mean.item() - 0.0684) < 1e-3

132	    def test_full_loop_with_set_alpha_to_one(self):
133	        # We specify different beta, so that the first alpha is 0.99
134	        sample = self.full_loop(set_alpha_to_one=True, beta_start=0.01)
135	        result_sum = torch.sum(torch.abs(sample))
136	        result_mean = torch.mean(torch.abs(sample))
137	
138	        assert abs(result_sum.item() - 149.8295) < 1e-2
139	        assert abs(result_mean.item() - 0.1951) < 1e-3

141	    def test_full_loop_with_no_set_alpha_to_one(self):
142	        # We specify different beta, so that the first alpha is 0.99
143	        sample = self.full_loop(set_alpha_to_one=False, beta_start=0.01)
144	        result_sum = torch.sum(torch.abs(sample))

150	    def test_full_loop_with_noise(self):
151	        scheduler_class = self.scheduler_classes[0]
152	        scheduler_config = self.get_scheduler_config()
153	        scheduler = scheduler_class(**scheduler_config)
154	
155	        num_inference_steps, eta = 10, 0.0
156	        t_start = 8
157	
158	        model = self.dummy_model()
159	        sample = self.dummy_sample_deter
160	
161	        scheduler.set_timesteps(num_inference_steps)
162	
163	        # add noise
164	        noise = self.dummy_noise_deter
165	        timesteps = scheduler.timesteps[t_start * scheduler.order :]
166	        sample = scheduler.add_noise(sample, noise, timesteps[:1])
167	
168	        for t in timesteps:
169	            residual = model(sample, t)
170	            sample = scheduler.step(residual, t, sample, eta).prev_sample
171	
172	        result_sum = torch.sum(torch.abs(sample))
173	        result_mean = torch.mean(torch.abs(sample))
174	
175	        assert abs(result_sum.item() - 354.5418) < 1e-2, f" expected result sum 218.4379, but get {result_sum}"
176	        assert abs(result_mean.item() - 0.4616) < 1e-3, f" expected result mean 0.2844, but get {result_mean}"

100	            cache_dir (`str | os.PathLike`, *optional*):
101	                Path to a directory in which a downloaded pretrained model configuration should be cached if the
102	                standard cache should not be used.

104	                Whether or not to force the (re-)download of the model weights and configuration files, overriding the
105	                cached versions if they exist.

107	            proxies (`dict[str, str]`, *optional*):

122	        > [!TIP] > It is required to be logged in (`hf auth login`) when you want to use private or [gated >

140	        scheduler, unused_kwargs = cls.from_config(config, return_unused_kwargs=True, **kwargs)
141

694	            if scheduler_class != VQDiffusionScheduler:
695	                sample = self.dummy_sample
696	                if scheduler_class == CMStochasticIterativeScheduler:
697	                    # Get valid timestep based on sigma_max, which should always be in timestep schedule.

699	                    scaled_sample = scheduler.scale_model_input(sample, scaled_sigma_max)
700	                elif scheduler_class == EDMEulerScheduler:

702	                else:

714	            sample = self.dummy_sample.to(torch_device)
715	            if scheduler_class == CMStochasticIterativeScheduler:

717	                scaled_sigma_max = scheduler.sigma_to_t(scheduler.config.sigma_max)
718	                scaled_sample = scheduler.scale_model_input(sample, scaled_sigma_max)

35	# Copied from diffusers.schedulers.scheduling_ddpm.DDPMSchedulerOutput with DDPM->EulerDiscrete
36	class EulerDiscreteSchedulerOutput(BaseOutput):
37	    """
38	    Output class for the scheduler's `step` function output.
39	
40	    Args:
41	        prev_sample (`torch.Tensor` of shape `(batch_size, num_channels, height, width)` for images):
42	            Computed sample `(x_{t-1})` of previous timestep. `prev_sample` should be used as next model input in the
43	            denoising loop.
44	        pred_original_sample (`torch.Tensor` of shape `(batch_size, num_channels, height, width)` for images):
45	            The predicted denoised sample `(x_{0})` based on the model output from the current timestep.
46	            `pred_original_sample` can be used to preview progress or for guidance.
47	    """
48	
49	    prev_sample: torch.Tensor
50	    pred_original_sample: torch.Tensor | None = None

53	# Copied from diffusers.schedulers.scheduling_ddpm.betas_for_alpha_bar
54	def betas_for_alpha_bar(
55	    num_diffusion_timesteps: int,
56	    max_beta: float = 0.999,
57	    alpha_transform_type: Literal["cosine", "exp", "laplace"] = "cosine",
58	) -> torch.Tensor:
59	    """
60	    Create a beta schedule that discretizes the given alpha_t_bar function, which defines the cumulative product of
61	    (1-beta) over time from t = [0,1].
62	
63	    Contains a function alpha_bar that takes an argument t and transforms it to the cumulative product of (1-beta) up
64	    to that part of the diffusion process.
65	
66	    Args:
67	        num_diffusion_timesteps (`int`):
68	            The number of betas to produce.
69	        max_beta (`float`, defaults to `0.999`):
70	            The maximum beta to use; use values lower than 1 to avoid numerical instability.
71	        alpha_transform_type (`str`, defaults to `"cosine"`):
72	            The type of noise schedule for `alpha_bar`. Choose from `cosine`, `exp`, or `laplace`.
73	
74	    Returns:
75	        `torch.Tensor`:
76	            The betas used by the scheduler to step the model outputs.
77	    """
78	    if alpha_transform_type == "cosine":
79	
80	        def alpha_bar_fn(t):
81	            return math.cos((t + 0.008) / 1.008 * math.pi / 2) ** 2
82	
83	    elif alpha_transform_type == "laplace":
84	
85	        def alpha_bar_fn(t):
86	            lmb = -0.5 * math.copysign(1, 0.5 - t) * math.log(1 - 2 * math.fabs(0.5 - t) + 1e-6)
87	            snr = math.exp(lmb)
88	            return math.sqrt(snr / (1 + snr))
89	
90	    elif alpha_transform_type == "exp":
91	
92	        def alpha_bar_fn(t):
93	            return math.exp(t * -12.0)
94	
95	    else:
96	        raise ValueError(f"Unsupported alpha_transform_type: {alpha_transform_type}")
97	
98	    betas = []
99	    for i in range(num_diffusion_timesteps):
100	        t1 = i / num_diffusion_timesteps
101	        t2 = (i + 1) / num_diffusion_timesteps
102	        betas.append(min(1 - alpha_bar_fn(t2) / alpha_bar_fn(t1), max_beta))
103	    return torch.tensor(betas, dtype=torch.float32)
104	
105	
106	# Copied from diffusers.schedulers.scheduling_ddim.rescale_zero_terminal_snr
107	def rescale_zero_terminal_snr(betas: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
108	    """
109	    Rescales betas to have zero terminal SNR Based on https://huggingface.co/papers/2305.08891 (Algorithm 1)
110	
111	    Args:
112	        betas (`torch.Tensor`):
113	            The betas that the scheduler is being initialized with.
114	
115	    Returns:
116	        `torch.Tensor`:
117	            Rescaled betas with zero terminal SNR.
118	    """
119	    # Convert betas to alphas_bar_sqrt
120	    alphas = 1.0 - betas
121	    alphas_cumprod = torch.cumprod(alphas, dim=0)
122	    alphas_bar_sqrt = alphas_cumprod.sqrt()
123	
124	    # Store old values.
125	    alphas_bar_sqrt_0 = alphas_bar_sqrt[0].clone()
126	    alphas_bar_sqrt_T = alphas_bar_sqrt[-1].clone()
127	
128	    # Shift so the last timestep is zero.
129	    alphas_bar_sqrt -= alphas_bar_sqrt_T
130	
131	    # Scale so the first timestep is back to the old value.
132	    alphas_bar_sqrt *= alphas_bar_sqrt_0 / (alphas_bar_sqrt_0 - alphas_bar_sqrt_T)
133	
134	    # Convert alphas_bar_sqrt to betas
135	    alphas_bar = alphas_bar_sqrt**2  # Revert sqrt
136	    alphas = alphas_bar[1:] / alphas_bar[:-1]  # Revert cumprod
137	    alphas = torch.cat([alphas_bar[0:1], alphas])
138	    betas = 1 - alphas
139	
140	    return betas

143	class EulerDiscreteScheduler(SchedulerMixin, ConfigMixin):
144	    """
145	    Euler scheduler.
146	
147	    This model inherits from [`SchedulerMixin`] and [`ConfigMixin`]. Check the superclass documentation for the generic
148	    methods the library implements for all schedulers such as loading and saving.
149	
150	    Args:
151	        num_train_timesteps (`int`, defaults to 1000):
152	            The number of diffusion steps to train the model.
153	        beta_start (`float`, defaults to 0.0001):
154	            The starting `beta` value of inference.
155	        beta_end (`float`, defaults to 0.02):
156	            The final `beta` value.
157	        beta_schedule (`Literal["linear", "scaled_linear", "squaredcos_cap_v2"]`, defaults to `"linear"`):
158	            The beta schedule, a mapping from a beta range to a sequence of betas for stepping the model. Choose from
159	            `"linear"`, `"scaled_linear"`, or `"squaredcos_cap_v2"`.
160	        trained_betas (`np.ndarray`, *optional*):
161	            Pass an array of betas directly to the constructor to bypass `beta_start` and `beta_end`.
162	        prediction_type (`Literal["epsilon", "sample", "v_prediction"]`, defaults to `"epsilon"`, *optional*):
163	            Prediction type of the scheduler function; can be `"epsilon"` (predicts the noise of the diffusion
164	            process), `"sample"` (directly predicts the noisy sample`) or `"v_prediction"` (see section 2.4 of [Imagen
165	            Video](https://huggingface.co/papers/2210.02303) paper).
166	        interpolation_type (`Literal["linear", "log_linear"]`, defaults to `"linear"`, *optional*):
167	            The interpolation type to compute intermediate sigmas for the scheduler denoising steps. Should be one of
168	            `"linear"` or `"log_linear"`.
169	        use_karras_sigmas (`bool`, *optional*, defaults to `False`):
170	            Whether to use Karras sigmas for step sizes in the noise schedule during the sampling process. If `True`,
171	            the sigmas are determined according to a sequence of noise levels {σi}.
172	        use_exponential_sigmas (`bool`, *optional*, defaults to `False`):
173	            Whether to use exponential sigmas for step sizes in the noise schedule during the sampling process.
174	        use_beta_sigmas (`bool`, *optional*, defaults to `False`):
175	            Whether to use beta sigmas for step sizes in the noise schedule during the sampling process. Refer to [Beta
176	            Sampling is All You Need](https://huggingface.co/papers/2407.12173) for more information.
177	        sigma_min (`float`, *optional*):
178	            The minimum sigma value for the noise schedule. If not provided, defaults to the last sigma in the
179	            schedule.
180	        sigma_max (`float`, *optional*):
181	            The maximum sigma value for the noise schedule. If not provided, defaults to the first sigma in the
182	            schedule.
183	        timestep_spacing (`Literal["linspace", "leading", "trailing"]`, defaults to `"linspace"`):
184	            The way the timesteps should be scaled. Refer to Table 2 of the [Common Diffusion Noise Schedules and
185	            Sample Steps are Flawed](https://huggingface.co/papers/2305.08891) for more information.
186	        timestep_type (`Literal["discrete", "continuous"]`, defaults to `"discrete"`):
187	            The type of timesteps to use. Can be `"discrete"` or `"continuous"`.
188	        steps_offset (`int`, defaults to 0):
189	            An offset added to the inference steps, as required by some model families.
190	        rescale_betas_zero_snr (`bool`, defaults to `False`):
191	            Whether to rescale the betas to have zero terminal SNR. This enables the model to generate very bright and
192	            dark samples instead of limiting it to samples with medium brightness. Loosely related to
193	            [`--offset_noise`](https://github.com/huggingface/diffusers/blob/74fd735eb073eb1d774b1ab4154a0876eb82f055/examples/dreambooth/train_dreambooth.py#L506).
194	        final_sigmas_type (`Literal["zero", "sigma_min"]`, defaults to `"zero"`):
195	            The final `sigma` value for the noise schedule during the sampling process. If `"sigma_min"`, the final
196	            sigma is the same as the last sigma in the training schedule. If `"zero"`, the final sigma is set to 0.
197	    """
198	
199	    _compatibles = [e.name for e in KarrasDiffusionSchedulers]
200	    order = 1

204	        self,

22	class DDPMParallelSchedulerTest(SchedulerCommonTest):
23	    scheduler_classes = (DDPMParallelScheduler,)
24	
25	    def get_scheduler_config(self, **kwargs):
26	        config = {
27	            "num_train_timesteps": 1000,
28	            "beta_start": 0.0001,
29	            "beta_end": 0.02,
30	            "beta_schedule": "linear",
31	            "variance_type": "fixed_small",
32	            "clip_sample": True,
33	        }
34	
35	        config.update(**kwargs)
36	        return config
37	
38	    def test_timesteps(self):
39	        for timesteps in [1, 5, 100, 1000]:
40	            self.check_over_configs(num_train_timesteps=timesteps)
41	
42	    def test_betas(self):
43	        for beta_start, beta_end in zip([0.0001, 0.001, 0.01, 0.1], [0.002, 0.02, 0.2, 2]):
44	            self.check_over_configs(beta_start=beta_start, beta_end=beta_end)
45	
46	    def test_schedules(self):
47	        for schedule in ["linear", "squaredcos_cap_v2"]:
48	            self.check_over_configs(beta_schedule=schedule)
49	
50	    def test_variance_type(self):
51	        for variance in ["fixed_small", "fixed_large", "other"]:
52	            self.check_over_configs(variance_type=variance)
53	
54	    def test_clip_sample(self):
55	        for clip_sample in [True, False]:
56	            self.check_over_configs(clip_sample=clip_sample)
57	
58	    def test_thresholding(self):
59	        self.check_over_configs(thresholding=False)

62	                self.check_over_configs(

66	                )

68	    def test_prediction_type(self):

72	    def test_time_indices(self):

76	    def test_variance(self):

81	        assert torch.sum(torch.abs(scheduler._get_variance(0) - 0.0)) < 1e-5

83	        assert torch.sum(torch.abs(scheduler._get_variance(999) - 0.02)) < 1e-5
84

89	    def test_batch_step_no_noise(self):
90	        scheduler_class = self.scheduler_classes[0]
91	        scheduler_config = self.get_scheduler_config()
92	        scheduler = scheduler_class(**scheduler_config)
93	
94	        num_trained_timesteps = len(scheduler)
95	
96	        model = self.dummy_model()
97	        sample1 = self.dummy_sample_deter
98	        sample2 = self.dummy_sample_deter + 0.1
99	        sample3 = self.dummy_sample_deter - 0.1
100	
101	        per_sample_batch = sample1.shape[0]
102	        samples = torch.stack([sample1, sample2, sample3], dim=0)
103	        timesteps = torch.arange(num_trained_timesteps)[0:3, None].repeat(1, per_sample_batch)
104	
105	        residual = model(samples.flatten(0, 1), timesteps.flatten(0, 1))
106	        pred_prev_sample = scheduler.batch_step_no_noise(residual, timesteps.flatten(0, 1), samples.flatten(0, 1))
107	
108	        result_sum = torch.sum(torch.abs(pred_prev_sample))
109	        result_mean = torch.mean(torch.abs(pred_prev_sample))
110	
111	        assert abs(result_sum.item() - 1153.1833) < 1e-2
112	        assert abs(result_mean.item() - 0.5005) < 1e-3
113	
114	    def test_full_loop_no_noise(self):
115	        scheduler_class = self.scheduler_classes[0]
116	        scheduler_config = self.get_scheduler_config()
117	        scheduler = scheduler_class(**scheduler_config)
118	
119	        num_trained_timesteps = len(scheduler)
120	
121	        model = self.dummy_model()
122	        sample = self.dummy_sample_deter
123	        generator = torch.manual_seed(0)
124	
125	        for t in reversed(range(num_trained_timesteps)):
126	            # 1. predict noise residual
127	            residual = model(sample, t)
128	
129	            # 2. predict previous mean of sample x_t-1
130	            pred_prev_sample = scheduler.step(residual, t, sample, generator=generator).prev_sample
131	
132	            sample = pred_prev_sample
133	
134	        result_sum = torch.sum(torch.abs(sample))
135	        result_mean = torch.mean(torch.abs(sample))
136	
137	        assert abs(result_sum.item() - 258.9606) < 1e-2
138	        assert abs(result_mean.item() - 0.3372) < 1e-3
139	
140	    def test_full_loop_with_v_prediction(self):

143	        scheduler = scheduler_class(**scheduler_config)
144	
145	        num_trained_timesteps = len(scheduler)
146	
147	        model = self.dummy_model()

149	        generator = torch.manual_seed(0)
150	
151	        for t in reversed(range(num_trained_timesteps)):
152	            # 1. predict noise residual
153	            residual = model(sample, t)
154	
155	            # 2. predict previous mean of sample x_t-1
156	            pred_prev_sample = scheduler.step(residual, t, sample, generator=generator).prev_sample
157	
158	            sample = pred_prev_sample
159	
160	        result_sum = torch.sum(torch.abs(sample))

163	        assert abs(result_sum.item() - 202.0296) < 1e-2

166	    def test_custom_timesteps(self):
167	        scheduler_class = self.scheduler_classes[0]
168	        scheduler_config = self.get_scheduler_config()
169	        scheduler = scheduler_class(**scheduler_config)
170	
171	        timesteps = [100, 87, 50, 1, 0]
172	
173	        scheduler.set_timesteps(timesteps=timesteps)
174	
175	        scheduler_timesteps = scheduler.timesteps
176	
177	        for i, timestep in enumerate(scheduler_timesteps):
178	            if i == len(timesteps) - 1:
179	                expected_prev_t = -1
180	            else:
181	                expected_prev_t = timesteps[i + 1]
182	
183	            prev_t = scheduler.previous_timestep(timestep)
184	            prev_t = prev_t.item()
185	
186	            self.assertEqual(prev_t, expected_prev_t)

222	    def test_full_loop_with_noise(self):
223	        scheduler_class = self.scheduler_classes[0]
224	        scheduler_config = self.get_scheduler_config()
225	        scheduler = scheduler_class(**scheduler_config)
226

228	        t_start = num_trained_timesteps - 2
229	
230	        model = self.dummy_model()

232	        generator = torch.manual_seed(0)
233	
234	        # add noise
235	        noise = self.dummy_noise_deter
236	        timesteps = scheduler.timesteps[t_start * scheduler.order :]
237	        sample = scheduler.add_noise(sample, noise, timesteps[:1])
238	
239	        for t in timesteps:
240	            # 1. predict noise residual
241	            residual = model(sample, t)
242	
243	            # 2. predict previous mean of sample x_t-1
244	            pred_prev_sample = scheduler.step(residual, t, sample, generator=generator).prev_sample
245	            sample = pred_prev_sample
246	
247	        result_sum = torch.sum(torch.abs(sample))
248	        result_mean = torch.mean(torch.abs(sample))
249	
250	        assert abs(result_sum.item() - 387.9466) < 1e-2, f" expected result sum 387.9466, but get {result_sum}"
251	        assert abs(result_mean.item() - 0.5051) < 1e-3, f" expected result mean 0.5051, but get {result_mean}"

32	# Copied from diffusers.schedulers.scheduling_ddpm.DDPMSchedulerOutput with DDPM->DDIM
33	class DDIMSchedulerOutput(BaseOutput):
34	    """
35	    Output class for the scheduler's `step` function output.
36	
37	    Args:
38	        prev_sample (`torch.Tensor` of shape `(batch_size, num_channels, height, width)` for images):
39	            Computed sample `(x_{t-1})` of previous timestep. `prev_sample` should be used as next model input in the
40	            denoising loop.
41	        pred_original_sample (`torch.Tensor` of shape `(batch_size, num_channels, height, width)` for images):
42	            The predicted denoised sample `(x_{0})` based on the model output from the current timestep.
43	            `pred_original_sample` can be used to preview progress or for guidance.
44	    """
45	
46	    prev_sample: torch.Tensor
47	    pred_original_sample: torch.Tensor | None = None
48

50	# Copied from diffusers.schedulers.scheduling_ddpm.betas_for_alpha_bar
51	def betas_for_alpha_bar(
52	    num_diffusion_timesteps: int,
53	    max_beta: float = 0.999,
54	    alpha_transform_type: Literal["cosine", "exp", "laplace"] = "cosine",
55	) -> torch.Tensor:
56	    """
57	    Create a beta schedule that discretizes the given alpha_t_bar function, which defines the cumulative product of
58	    (1-beta) over time from t = [0,1].
59	
60	    Contains a function alpha_bar that takes an argument t and transforms it to the cumulative product of (1-beta) up
61	    to that part of the diffusion process.
62	
63	    Args:
64	        num_diffusion_timesteps (`int`):
65	            The number of betas to produce.
66	        max_beta (`float`, defaults to `0.999`):
67	            The maximum beta to use; use values lower than 1 to avoid numerical instability.
68	        alpha_transform_type (`str`, defaults to `"cosine"`):
69	            The type of noise schedule for `alpha_bar`. Choose from `cosine`, `exp`, or `laplace`.
70	
71	    Returns:
72	        `torch.Tensor`:
73	            The betas used by the scheduler to step the model outputs.
74	    """
75	    if alpha_transform_type == "cosine":
76	
77	        def alpha_bar_fn(t):
78	            return math.cos((t + 0.008) / 1.008 * math.pi / 2) ** 2
79	
80	    elif alpha_transform_type == "laplace":

223	    def from_config(
224	        cls, config: FrozenDict | dict[str, Any] = None, return_unused_kwargs=False, **kwargs
225	    ) -> Self | tuple[Self, dict[str, Any]]:
226	        r"""
227	        Instantiate a Python class from a config dictionary.
228	
229	        Parameters:
230	            config (`dict[str, Any]`):
231	                A config dictionary from which the Python class is instantiated. Make sure to only load configuration
232	                files of compatible classes.
233	            return_unused_kwargs (`bool`, *optional*, defaults to `False`):
234	                Whether kwargs that are not consumed by the Python class should be returned or not.
235	            kwargs (remaining dictionary of keyword arguments, *optional*):
236	                Can be used to update the configuration object (after it is loaded) and initiate the Python class.
237	                `**kwargs` are passed directly to the underlying scheduler/model's `__init__` method and eventually
238	                overwrite the same named arguments in `config`.
239	
240	        Returns:
241	            [`ModelMixin`] or [`SchedulerMixin`]:
242	                A model or scheduler object instantiated from a config dictionary.
243	
244	        Examples:
245	
246	        ```python
247	        >>> from diffusers import DDPMScheduler, DDIMScheduler, PNDMScheduler
248	
249	        >>> # Download scheduler from huggingface.co and cache.
250	        >>> scheduler = DDPMScheduler.from_pretrained("google/ddpm-cifar10-32")
251	
252	        >>> # Instantiate DDIM scheduler class with same config as DDPM
253	        >>> scheduler = DDIMScheduler.from_config(scheduler.config)
254	
255	        >>> # Instantiate PNDM scheduler class with same config as DDPM
256	        >>> scheduler = PNDMScheduler.from_config(scheduler.config)
257	        ```
258	        """
259	        # <===== TO BE REMOVED WITH DEPRECATION
260	        # TODO(Patrick) - make sure to remove the following lines when config=="model_path" is deprecated
261	        if "pretrained_model_name_or_path" in kwargs:
262	            config = kwargs.pop("pretrained_model_name_or_path")
263	
264	        if config is None:
265	            raise ValueError("Please make sure to provide a config as the first positional argument.")
266	        # ======>
267	
268	        if not isinstance(config, dict):
269	            deprecation_message = "It is deprecated to pass a pretrained model name or path to `from_config`."
270	            if "Scheduler" in cls.__name__:
271	                deprecation_message += (
272	                    f"If you were trying to load a scheduler, please use {cls}.from_pretrained(...) instead."
273	                    " Otherwise, please make sure to pass a configuration dictionary instead. This functionality will"
274	                    " be removed in v1.0.0."
275	                )
276	            elif "Model" in cls.__name__:
277	                deprecation_message += (
278	                    f"If you were trying to load a model, please use {cls}.load_config(...) followed by"
279	                    f" {cls}.from_config(...) instead. Otherwise, please make sure to pass a configuration dictionary"
280	                    " instead. This functionality will be removed in v1.0.0."
281	                )
282	            deprecate("config-passed-as-path", "1.0.0", deprecation_message, standard_warn=False)
283	            config, kwargs = cls.load_config(pretrained_model_name_or_path=config, return_unused_kwargs=True, **kwargs)
284	
285	        init_dict, unused_kwargs, hidden_dict = cls.extract_init_dict(config, **kwargs)
286	
287	        # Allow dtype to be specified on initialization
288	        if "dtype" in unused_kwargs:
289	            init_dict["dtype"] = unused_kwargs.pop("dtype")
290	
291	        # add possible deprecated kwargs
292	        for deprecated_kwarg in cls._deprecated_kwargs:
293	            if deprecated_kwarg in unused_kwargs:
294	                init_dict[deprecated_kwarg] = unused_kwargs.pop(deprecated_kwarg)
295	
296	        # Return model and optionally state and/or unused_kwargs
297	        model = cls(**init_dict)
298	
299	        # make sure to also save config parameters that might be used for compatible classes
300	        # update _class_name
301	        if "_class_name" in hidden_dict:
302	            hidden_dict["_class_name"] = cls.__name__
303	
304	        model.register_to_config(**hidden_dict)
305	
306	        # add hidden kwargs of compatible classes to unused_kwargs
307	        unused_kwargs = {**unused_kwargs, **hidden_dict}
308	
309	        if return_unused_kwargs:
310	            return (model, unused_kwargs)
311	        else:
312	            return model

317	            f" The function get_config_dict is deprecated. Please use {cls}.load_config instead. This function will be"
318	            " removed in version v1.0.0"
319	        )
320	        deprecate("get_config_dict", "1.0.0", deprecation_message, standard_warn=False)
321	        return cls.load_config(*args, **kwargs)

class DDIMScheduler(SchedulerMixin, ConfigMixin):
    """
    `DDIMScheduler` extends the denoising procedure introduced in denoising diffusion probabilistic models (DDPMs) with
    non-Markovian guidance.

    This model inherits from [`SchedulerMixin`] and [`ConfigMixin`]. Check the superclass documentation for the generic
tests/schedulers/test_schedulers.py:772:            scheduler.register_to_config(test_attribute=5)

110	
111	    Returns:
112	        `torch.Tensor`:
113	            Rescaled betas with zero terminal SNR.
114	    """
115	    # Convert betas to alphas_bar_sqrt
116	    alphas = 1.0 - betas
117	    alphas_cumprod = torch.cumprod(alphas, dim=0)
118	    alphas_bar_sqrt = alphas_cumprod.sqrt()
119	
120	    # Store old values.
121	    alphas_bar_sqrt_0 = alphas_bar_sqrt[0].clone()
122	    alphas_bar_sqrt_T = alphas_bar_sqrt[-1].clone()
123	
124	    # Shift so the last timestep is zero.
125	    alphas_bar_sqrt -= alphas_bar_sqrt_T
126	
127	    # Scale so the first timestep is back to the old value.
128	    alphas_bar_sqrt *= alphas_bar_sqrt_0 / (alphas_bar_sqrt_0 - alphas_bar_sqrt_T)
129	
130	    # Convert alphas_bar_sqrt to betas
131	    alphas_bar = alphas_bar_sqrt**2  # Revert sqrt
132	    alphas = alphas_bar[1:] / alphas_bar[:-1]  # Revert cumprod
133	    alphas = torch.cat([alphas_bar[0:1], alphas])
134	    betas = 1 - alphas
135	
136	    return betas

140	    """
141	    `DDIMScheduler` extends the denoising procedure introduced in denoising diffusion probabilistic models (DDPMs) with
142	    non-Markovian guidance.
143	
144	    This model inherits from [`SchedulerMixin`] and [`ConfigMixin`]. Check the superclass documentation for the generic
145	    methods the library implements for all schedulers such as loading and saving.
146	
147	    Args:
148	        num_train_timesteps (`int`, defaults to 1000):
149	            The number of diffusion steps to train the model.
150	        beta_start (`float`, defaults to 0.0001):
151	            The starting `beta` value of inference.
152	        beta_end (`float`, defaults to 0.02):
153	            The final `beta` value.
154	        beta_schedule (`Literal["linear", "scaled_linear", "squaredcos_cap_v2"]`, defaults to `"linear"`):
155	            The beta schedule, a mapping from a beta range to a sequence of betas for stepping the model. Must be one
156	            of `"linear"`, `"scaled_linear"`, or `"squaredcos_cap_v2"`.
157	        trained_betas (`np.ndarray`, *optional*):
158	            Pass an array of betas directly to the constructor to bypass `beta_start` and `beta_end`.
159	        clip_sample (`bool`, defaults to `True`):
160	            Clip the predicted sample for numerical stability.
161	        clip_sample_range (`float`, defaults to 1.0):
162	            The maximum magnitude for sample clipping. Valid only when `clip_sample=True`.
163	        set_alpha_to_one (`bool`, defaults to `True`):
164	            Each diffusion step uses the alphas product value at that step and at the previous one. For the final step
165	            there is no previous alpha. When this option is `True` the previous alpha product is fixed to `1`,
166	            otherwise it uses the alpha value at step 0.
167	        steps_offset (`int`, defaults to 0):
168	            An offset added to the inference steps, as required by some model families.
169	        prediction_type (`Literal["epsilon", "sample", "v_prediction"]`, defaults to `"epsilon"`):
170	            Prediction type of the scheduler function. Must be one of `"epsilon"` (predicts the noise of the diffusion
171	            process), `"sample"` (directly predicts the noisy sample), or `"v_prediction"` (see section 2.4 of [Imagen
172	            Video](https://huggingface.co/papers/2210.02303) paper).
173	        thresholding (`bool`, defaults to `False`):
174	            Whether to use the "dynamic thresholding" method. This is unsuitable for latent-space diffusion models such
175	            as Stable Diffusion.
176	        dynamic_thresholding_ratio (`float`, defaults to 0.995):
177	            The ratio for the dynamic thresholding method. Valid only when `thresholding=True`.
178	        sample_max_value (`float`, defaults to 1.0):
179	            The threshold value for dynamic thresholding. Valid only when `thresholding=True`.
180	        timestep_spacing (`Literal["leading", "trailing", "linspace"]`, defaults to `"leading"`):
181	            The way the timesteps should be scaled. Must be one of `"leading"`, `"trailing"`, or `"linspace"`. Refer to
182	            Table 2 of the [Common Diffusion Noise Schedules and Sample Steps are
183	            Flawed](https://huggingface.co/papers/2305.08891) for more information.
184	        rescale_betas_zero_snr (`bool`, defaults to `False`):
185	            Whether to rescale the betas to have zero terminal SNR. This enables the model to generate very bright and
186	            dark samples instead of limiting it to samples with medium brightness. Loosely related to
187	            [`--offset_noise`](https://github.com/huggingface/diffusers/blob/74fd735eb073eb1d774b1ab4154a0876eb82f055/examples/dreambooth/train_dreambooth.py#L506).
188	    """

193	    @register_to_config
194	    def __init__(
195	        self,
196	        num_train_timesteps: int = 1000,
197	        beta_start: float = 0.0001,
198	        beta_end: float = 0.02,
199	        beta_schedule: str = "linear",
200	        trained_betas: np.ndarray | list[float] | None = None,
201	        clip_sample: bool = True,
202	        set_alpha_to_one: bool = True,
203	        steps_offset: int = 0,
204	        prediction_type: Literal["epsilon", "sample", "v_prediction"] = "epsilon",
205	        thresholding: bool = False,
206	        dynamic_thresholding_ratio: float = 0.995,
207	        clip_sample_range: float = 1.0,
208	        sample_max_value: float = 1.0,
209	        timestep_spacing: Literal["leading", "trailing", "linspace"] = "leading",
210	        rescale_betas_zero_snr: bool = False,
211	    ):
212	        if trained_betas is not None:
213	            self.betas = torch.tensor(trained_betas, dtype=torch.float32)
214	        elif beta_schedule == "linear":
215	            self.betas = torch.linspace(beta_start, beta_end, num_train_timesteps, dtype=torch.float32)
216	        elif beta_schedule == "scaled_linear":
217	            # this schedule is very specific to the latent diffusion model.
218	            self.betas = torch.linspace(beta_start**0.5, beta_end**0.5, num_train_timesteps, dtype=torch.float32) ** 2
219	        elif beta_schedule == "squaredcos_cap_v2":
220	            # Glide cosine schedule
221	            self.betas = betas_for_alpha_bar(num_train_timesteps)
222	        else:
223	            raise NotImplementedError(f"{beta_schedule} is not implemented for {self.__class__}")
224	
225	        # Rescale for zero SNR
226	        if rescale_betas_zero_snr:
227	            self.betas = rescale_zero_terminal_snr(self.betas)
228	
229	        self.alphas = 1.0 - self.betas
230	        self.alphas_cumprod = torch.cumprod(self.alphas, dim=0)
231	
232	        # At every step in ddim, we are looking into the previous alphas_cumprod
233	        # For the final step, there is no previous alphas_cumprod because we are already at 0
234	        # `set_alpha_to_one` decides whether we set this parameter simply to one or
235	        # whether we use the final alpha of the "non-previous" one.
236	        self.final_alpha_cumprod = torch.tensor(1.0) if set_alpha_to_one else self.alphas_cumprod[0]
237	
238	        # standard deviation of the initial noise distribution
239	        self.init_noise_sigma = 1.0
240	
241	        # setable values
242	        self.num_inference_steps = None
243	        self.timesteps = torch.from_numpy(np.arange(0, num_train_timesteps)[::-1].copy().astype(np.int64))

Perfect. Now I have all the information I need to provide a comprehensive report. Let me compile it.

## COMPREHENSIVE SCHEDULER IMPLEMENTATION ANALYSIS

I've completed a thorough exploration of the Hugging Face diffusers scheduler implementation. Here's the complete technical breakdown:

### 1. SCHEDULER FILE ORGANIZATION

**Location:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775499384049-cb77l1/src/diffusers/schedulers/`

**Total Scheduler Files:** 54 scheduler implementations with the following structure:

#### Core Schedulers
- `scheduling_ddpm.py` - Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Models (DDPM)
- `scheduling_ddim.py` - Denoising Diffusion Implicit Models (DDIM)
- `scheduling_euler_discrete.py` - Euler method scheduler
- `scheduling_pndm.py` - Pseudo Numerical Method for Diffusion
- `scheduling_lms_discrete.py` - Linear Multistep Discrete

#### Advanced/Specialized Schedulers
- DPM Solver variants (single/multi-step, SDE)
- Heun discrete, KDPM2 variants
- Flow matching variants (Euler, Heun, LCM)
- EDM schedulers (Euler, DPM Solver)
- UniPC Multistep
- And 30+ more specialized implementations

#### Flax (JAX) Implementations
- `scheduling_ddim_flax.py`, `scheduling_ddpm_flax.py`, etc.
- `scheduling_utils_flax.py` - Flax base utilities

#### Utilities
- `scheduling_utils.py` - Core PyTorch base classes
- `scheduling_utils_flax.py` - Core Flax base classes

---

### 2. BASE CLASSES & INHERITANCE HIERARCHY

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775499384049-cb77l1/src/diffusers/schedulers/scheduling_utils.py`

```
SchedulerMixin (base class)
├── Inherits from: PushToHubMixin
├── Key Attributes:
│   ├── config_name = "scheduler_config.json"
│   ├── _compatibles = []  # list of compatible scheduler names
│   ├── has_compatibles = True
│   └── order = 1  # execution order in pipelines
├── Key Methods:
│   ├── from_pretrained(pretrained_model_name_or_path, subfolder, return_unused_kwargs, **kwargs)
│   │   └── Returns: Self (scheduler instance)
│   ├── save_pretrained(save_directory, push_to_hub=False, **kwargs)
│   ├── @property compatibles -> list[SchedulerMixin]
│   └── _get_compatibles() -> list[class]
```

**SchedulerOutput Dataclass:**
```python
@dataclass
class SchedulerOutput(BaseOutput):
    prev_sample: torch.Tensor  # shape (batch_size, num_channels, height, width)
```

---

### 3. ConfigMixin INTEGRATION

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775499384049-cb77l1/src/diffusers/configuration_utils.py`

All schedulers inherit from both `SchedulerMixin` AND `ConfigMixin`:

```python
class DDPMScheduler(SchedulerMixin, ConfigMixin):
    @register_to_config
    def __init__(self, ...):
        pass
```

**ConfigMixin Features:**
- Stores all init parameters in `self._internal_dict` (a FrozenDict)
- Accessed via `scheduler.config.parameter_name`
- Loaded/saved automatically with JSON serialization
- `config_name` specifies the JSON filename (default: "scheduler_config.json")

**@register_to_config Decorator (Line 690):**
```python
def register_to_config(init):
    # Wraps __init__ to automatically capture all parameters
    # Filters out private kwargs (starting with _)
    # Calls self.register_to_config(**kwargs) internally

2. **"linear":** `torch.linspace(beta_start, beta_end, num_train_timesteps)`

self.alphas = 1.0 - self.betas

```

def step(
    self,

    sample: torch.Tensor,  # x_t (noisy sample)
    generator: torch.Generator | None = None,
    return_dict: bool = True,
) -> DDPMSchedulerOutput | tuple:
```

**Steps:**
1. **Get alphas** for current and previous timesteps
2. **Predict original sample** (x_0) based on prediction_type:
   - "epsilon": `(x_t - sqrt(beta_prod_t) * model_output) / sqrt(alpha_prod_t)`
   - "sample": Use model_output directly
   - "v_prediction": `sqrt(alpha_prod_t) * x_t - sqrt(beta_prod_t) * model_output`
3. **Clip/threshold** if configured
4. **Compute coefficients** for prediction and sample contribution
5. **Compute mean** of previous sample distribution
6. **Add noise** (if not at final step):
   - Get variance based on variance_type
   - Sample noise and scale by variance
7. **Return:** DDPMSchedulerOutput with prev_sample and pred_original_sample

**Signature:**
```python

    self,

    timesteps: torch.IntTensor,  # which noise levels
) -> torch.Tensor:
```

**Implementation:**
```python
sqrt_alpha_prod = alphas_cumprod[timesteps] ** 0.5
sqrt_one_minus_alpha_prod = (1 - alphas_cumprod[timesteps]) ** 0.5
noisy_samples = sqrt_alpha_prod * original_samples + sqrt_one_minus_alpha_prod * noise
# Formula: x_t = sqrt(alpha_bar_t) * x_0 + sqrt(1 - alpha_bar_t) * noise
```

**Purpose:** Compute velocity prediction for v_prediction variant

**Formula:**

velocity = sqrt_alpha_prod * noise - sqrt_one_minus_alpha_prod * sample
```

### 5. BETA SCHEDULE FUNCTIONS

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775499384049-cb77l1/src/diffusers/schedulers/scheduling_ddpm.py` (Lines 48-97)

#### betas_for_alpha_bar Function

**Signature:**
```python
def betas_for_alpha_bar(
    num_diffusion_timesteps: int,
    max_beta: float = 0.999,
    alpha_transform_type: Literal["cosine", "exp", "laplace"] = "cosine",
) -> torch.Tensor:
```

**Alpha Bar Functions:**
1. **"cosine":**
   ```python
   alpha_bar_fn(t) = cos((t + 0.008) / 1.008 * π/2)^2
   ```

2. **"laplace":**
   ```python
   lmb = -0.5 * sign(0.5 - t) * log(1 - 2|0.5 - t| + 1e-6)
   snr = exp(lmb)
   alpha_bar_fn(t) = sqrt(snr / (1 + snr))
   ```

3. **"exp":**
   ```python
   alpha_bar_fn(t) = exp(t * -12.0)
   ```

**Beta Computation:**
```python
for i in range(num_diffusion_timesteps):
    t1 = i / num_diffusion_timesteps
    t2 = (i + 1) / num_diffusion_timesteps
    beta_i = min(1 - alpha_bar(t2) / alpha_bar(t1), max_beta)
```

#### rescale_zero_terminal_snr Function (Lines 100-134)

**Purpose:** Rescale betas to have zero terminal SNR (from paper 2305.08891)

**Algorithm:**
```python
alphas = 1.0 - betas
alphas_cumprod = cumprod(alphas)
alphas_bar_sqrt = sqrt(alphas_cumprod)

# Shift to zero terminal
alphas_bar_sqrt -= alphas_bar_sqrt[-1]

# Scale to restore initial value
alphas_bar_sqrt *= alphas_bar_sqrt[0] / (alphas_bar_sqrt[0] - alphas_bar_sqrt[-1])

# Convert back to betas
alphas_bar = alphas_bar_sqrt^2
alphas = alphas_bar[1:] / alphas_bar[:-1]
betas = 1 - alphas
```

- Handles: "fixed_small", "fixed_small_log", "fixed_large", "fixed_large_log", "learned", "learned_range"

**_threshold_sample (Lines 418-459):**
- Implements dynamic thresholding (percentile-based clipping)
- Prevents pixel saturation in predicted samples

**previous_timestep (Lines 648-668):**
- Computes previous timestep in the chain
- Handles both custom and equally-spaced timesteps

---

### 5. BETA SCHEDULE FUNCTIONS

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775499384049-cb77l1/src/diffusers/schedulers/scheduling_ddpm.py` (Lines 48-97)

#### betas_for_alpha_bar Function

**Signature:**
```python
def betas_for_alpha_bar(
    num_diffusion_timesteps: int,
    max_beta: float = 0.999,
    alpha_transform_type: Literal["cosine", "exp", "laplace"] = "cosine",
) -> torch.Tensor:
```

**Alpha Bar Functions:**
1. **"cosine":**
   ```python
   alpha_bar_fn(t) = cos((t + 0.008) / 1.008 * π/2)^2
   ```

2. **"laplace":**
   ```python
   lmb = -0.5 * sign(0.5 - t) * log(1 - 2|0.5 - t| + 1e-6)
   snr = exp(lmb)
   alpha_bar_fn(t) = sqrt(snr / (1 + snr))
   ```

3. **"exp":**
   ```python
   alpha_bar_fn(t) = exp(t * -12.0)
   ```

**Beta Computation:**
```python
for i in range(num_diffusion_timesteps):
    t1 = i / num_diffusion_timesteps
    t2 = (i + 1) / num_diffusion_timesteps
    beta_i = min(1 - alpha_bar(t2) / alpha_bar(t1), max_beta)
```

#### rescale_zero_terminal_snr Function (Lines 100-134)

**Purpose:** Rescale betas to have zero terminal SNR (from paper 2305.08891)

**Algorithm:**
```python
alphas = 1.0 - betas
alphas_cumprod = cumprod(alphas)
alphas_bar_sqrt = sqrt(alphas_cumprod)

# Shift to zero terminal
alphas_bar_sqrt -= alphas_bar_sqrt[-1]

# Scale to restore initial value
alphas_bar_sqrt *= alphas_bar_sqrt[0] / (alphas_bar_sqrt[0] - alphas_bar_sqrt[-1])

# Convert back to betas
alphas_bar = alphas_bar_sqrt^2
alphas = alphas_bar[1:] / alphas_bar[:-1]
betas = 1 - alphas
```

### 6. DDIM SCHEDULER - KEY DIFFERENCES

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775499384049-cb77l1/src/diffusers/schedulers/scheduling_ddim.py`

**Class:** `DDIMScheduler(SchedulerMixin, ConfigMixin)` (Line 139)

**Key Differences from DDPM:**

1. **set_alpha_to_one parameter:** Sets final alpha product to 1.0 instead of 0

2. **step method signature adds `eta` parameter:**
   ```python
   eta: float = 0.0  # 0=deterministic (DDIM), 1=stochastic (DDPM)
   ```

3. **Timestep spacing in set_timesteps:**
   - Uses step_ratio to downsample: `step_ratio = num_train_timesteps // num_inference_steps`
   - Creates sparser timestep sequences

4. **No variance_type complexity:** Only adds noise scaled by eta

5. **No add_noise/get_velocity methods:** Inherits shared beta schedule utilities

### 7. EULER DISCRETE SCHEDULER - ADVANCED FEATURES

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775499384049-cb77l1/src/diffusers/schedulers/scheduling_euler_discrete.py`

- `use_exponential_sigmas`: Exponential noise progression
- `use_beta_sigmas`: Beta-based sigmas (requires scipy)
- `sigma_min`, `sigma_max`: Custom sigma range
- `interpolation_type`: "linear" or "log_linear"
- `timestep_type`: "discrete" or "continuous"
- `final_sigmas_type`: "zero" or "sigma_min"

**Key Difference:** Uses sigma-based scheduling instead of alpha/beta

### 9. FLAX (JAX) SCHEDULER BASE

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775499384049-cb77l1/src/diffusers/schedulers/scheduling_utils_flax.py`

### 10. STANDARD SCHEDULER OUTPUT DATACLASS PATTERN

All schedulers define custom output classes inheriting from `BaseOutput`:

```python
@dataclass
class DDPMSchedulerOutput(BaseOutput):

    prev_sample: torch.Tensor  # Required: denoised sample x_{t-1}
    pred_original_sample: torch.Tensor | None = None  # Optional: predicted x_0

### 11. CONFIGURATION AND LOADING

**Config File Format:** JSON with all __init__ parameters

    "beta_start": 0.0001,
    "beta_end": 0.02,
    "beta_schedule": "linear",
    "variance_type": "fixed_small",
    "clip_sample": true,
    "prediction_type": "epsilon",
    "_class_name": "DDPMScheduler",
    "_diffusers_version": "0.27.0"
}
```

**Loading:**
```python
scheduler = DDPMScheduler.from_pretrained("model_id")

```

**Saving:**
```python
scheduler.save_pretrained("./scheduler_dir")
```

   - Other `dummy_*.py` files for other dependencies

### 8. **Key Design Patterns**

1. **Lazy Module Pattern**: Uses `_LazyModule` to avoid importing heavy dependencies on library load
2. **Dual Import Structure**: Separates lazy loading (`_import_structure`) from direct imports (TYPE_CHECKING)
3. **Dependency Injection**: Conditionally exports based on installed packages

### 4. **Simple Scheduler Test Pattern**

**Example**: `DDPMSchedulerTest` (test_scheduler_ddpm.py)

class DDPMSchedulerTest(SchedulerCommonTest):
    scheduler_classes = (DDPMScheduler,)
    
    def get_scheduler_config(self, **kwargs):
        config = {
            "num_train_timesteps": 1000,
            "beta_start": 0.0001,
            "beta_end": 0.02,
            "beta_schedule": "linear",
            "variance_type": "fixed_small",
            "clip_sample": True,
        }
        config.update(**kwargs)
        return config
    
    def test_timesteps(self):
        for timesteps in [1, 5, 100, 1000]:
            self.check_over_configs(num_train_timesteps=timesteps)
    
    def test_betas(self):
        for beta_start, beta_end in zip([0.0001, 0.001, 0.01, 0.1], 
                                        [0.002, 0.02, 0.2, 2]):
            self.check_over_configs(beta_start=beta_start, beta_end=beta_end)
```

### 5. **Scheduler with Forward Kwargs Pattern**

**Example**: `DDIMSchedulerTest` (test_scheduler_ddim.py)

Schedulers with required inference-time kwargs use `forward_default_kwargs`:

```python
class DDIMSchedulerTest(SchedulerCommonTest):
    scheduler_classes = (DDIMScheduler,)
    forward_default_kwargs = (("eta", 0.0), ("num_inference_steps", 50))
    
    def full_loop(self, **config):
        """Custom integration test method"""
        scheduler_class = self.scheduler_classes[0]
        scheduler_config = self.get_scheduler_config(**config)
        scheduler = scheduler_class(**scheduler_config)
        
        num_inference_steps, eta = 10, 0.0
        model = self.dummy_model()
        sample = self.dummy_sample_deter
        
        scheduler.set_timesteps(num_inference_steps)
        
        for t in scheduler.timesteps:
            residual = model(sample, t)
            sample = scheduler.step(residual, t, sample, eta).prev_sample
        
        return sample
    
    def test_full_loop_no_noise(self):
        sample = self.full_loop()
        result_sum = torch.sum(torch.abs(sample))
        result_mean = torch.mean(torch.abs(sample))
        
        assert abs(result_sum.item() - 172.0067) < 1e-2
        assert abs(result_mean.item() - 0.223967) < 1e-3
```

class DPMSolverMultistepSchedulerTest(SchedulerCommonTest):
    scheduler_classes = (DPMSolverMultistepScheduler,)
    forward_default_kwargs = (("num_inference_steps", 25),)
    
    def check_over_configs(self, time_step=0, **config):
        """Override to handle multi-step scheduler specifics"""
        kwargs = dict(self.forward_default_kwargs)
        num_inference_steps = kwargs.pop("num_inference_steps", None)
        sample = self.dummy_sample
        residual = 0.1 * sample
        dummy_past_residuals = [residual + 0.2, residual + 0.15, residual + 0.10]
        
        for scheduler_class in self.scheduler_classes:
            scheduler_config = self.get_scheduler_config(**config)
            scheduler = scheduler_class(**scheduler_config)
            scheduler.set_timesteps(num_inference_steps)
            scheduler.model_outputs = dummy_past_residuals[: scheduler.config.solver_order]
            # ... rest of test
```

### 7. **Custom Timestep/Sigma Tests Pattern**

```python
def full_loop_custom_timesteps(self, **config):
    """Test with custom timesteps instead of num_inference_steps"""
    scheduler_class = self.scheduler_classes[0]
    scheduler_config = self.get_scheduler_config(**config)
    scheduler = scheduler_class(**scheduler_config)
    
    num_inference_steps = self.num_inference_steps
    scheduler.set_timesteps(num_inference_steps)
    timesteps = scheduler.timesteps
    
    # Reset using custom timesteps
    scheduler = scheduler_class(**scheduler_config)
    scheduler.set_timesteps(num_inference_steps=None, timesteps=timesteps)
    
    generator = torch.manual_seed(0)
    model = self.dummy_model()
    sample = self.dummy_sample_deter * scheduler.init_noise_sigma
    
    for i, t in enumerate(scheduler.timesteps):
        sample = scheduler.scale_model_input(sample, t)
        model_output = model(sample, t)
        output = scheduler.step(model_output, t, sample, generator=generator)
        sample = output.prev_sample
    
    return sample

def test_full_loop_custom_timesteps(self):
    sample = self.full_loop_custom_timesteps()
    result_sum = torch.sum(torch.abs(sample))
    assert abs(result_sum.item() - EXPECTED_VALUE) < 1e-2
```

                thresholding=True,

            )
```

### 9. **Full Integration Tests**

**Pattern** (custom test methods):

```python
def test_full_loop_no_noise(self):
    scheduler_class = self.scheduler_classes[0]
    scheduler_config = self.get_scheduler_config()
    scheduler = scheduler_class(**scheduler_config)
    
    num_trained_timesteps = len(scheduler)
    
    model = self.dummy_model()
    sample = self.dummy_sample_deter
    generator = torch.manual_seed(0)
    
    for t in reversed(range(num_trained_timesteps)):
        residual = model(sample, t)
        pred_prev_sample = scheduler.step(residual, t, sample, 
                                         generator=generator).prev_sample
        sample = pred_prev_sample
    
    result_sum = torch.sum(torch.abs(sample))
    result_mean = torch.mean(torch.abs(sample))
    
    assert abs(result_sum.item() - 258.9606) < 1e-2
    assert abs(result_mean.item() - 0.3372) < 1e-3

def test_full_loop_with_noise(self):
    # Test with noise injection
    scheduler_class = self.scheduler_classes[0]
    scheduler_config = self.get_scheduler_config()
    scheduler = scheduler_class(**scheduler_config)
    
    num_trained_timesteps = len(scheduler)
    t_start = num_trained_timesteps - 2
    
    model = self.dummy_model()
    sample = self.dummy_sample_deter
    generator = torch.manual_seed(0)
    
    # Add noise
    noise = self.dummy_noise_deter
    timesteps = scheduler.timesteps[t_start * scheduler.order :]
    sample = scheduler.add_noise(sample, noise, timesteps[:1])
    
    for t in timesteps:
        residual = model(sample, t)
        pred_prev_sample = scheduler.step(residual, t, sample, 
                                         generator=generator).prev_sample
        sample = pred_prev_sample
    
    result_sum = torch.sum(torch.abs(sample))
    result_mean = torch.mean(torch.abs(sample))
    
    assert abs(result_sum.item() - 387.9466) < 1e-2
    assert abs(result_mean.item() - 0.5051) < 1e-3
```

### 12. **File Structure Summary**

├── test_schedulers.py          # SchedulerBaseTests, SchedulerCommonTest (868 lines)
├── test_scheduler_ddpm.py      # Simple single-scheduler test

```

### 13. **Key Testing Utilities**

From `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775499384049-cb77l1/tests/testing_utils.py`:

- `torch_device` - Device for tests (CPU/GPU/MPS)
- `CaptureLogger` - Capture logging output
- `assert_tensors_close()` - Tensor comparison with tolerance

### 14. **Pattern for Creating New Scheduler Tests**

**Minimal template**:

```python
import torch
from diffusers import MyNewScheduler
from .test_schedulers import SchedulerCommonTest

class MyNewSchedulerTest(SchedulerCommonTest):
    scheduler_classes = (MyNewScheduler,)
    # Optional: specify forward_default_kwargs if scheduler.step() requires specific kwargs
    forward_default_kwargs = ()
    
    def get_scheduler_config(self, **kwargs):
        """Required: return dict with all scheduler __init__ parameters"""
        config = {
            "num_train_timesteps": 1000,
            "beta_start": 0.0001,
            "beta_end": 0.02,
            "beta_schedule": "linear",
            # ... other required config params
        }
        config.update(**kwargs)
        return config
    
    # Add scheduler-specific config parameter tests
    def test_beta_schedule(self):
        for schedule in ["linear", "scaled_linear"]:
            self.check_over_configs(beta_schedule=schedule)
    
    def test_prediction_type(self):
        for prediction_type in ["epsilon", "v_prediction"]:
            self.check_over_configs(prediction_type=prediction_type)
    
    # Optional: Add integration test
    def test_full_loop_no_noise(self):
        scheduler_class = self.scheduler_classes[0]
        scheduler_config = self.get_scheduler_config()
        scheduler = scheduler_class(**scheduler_config)
        
        model = self.dummy_model()
        sample = self.dummy_sample_deter
        
        scheduler.set_timesteps(50)  # or however your scheduler handles steps
        
        for t in scheduler.timesteps:
            residual = model(sample, t)
            sample = scheduler.step(residual, t, sample).prev_sample
        
        # Verify output with known good values
        result_sum = torch.sum(torch.abs(sample))
        assert abs(result_sum.item() - EXPECTED_VALUE) < 1e-2
```

### 15. **Important Conventions**

1. **Deterministic testing**: Use `torch.manual_seed(0)` and `self.dummy_sample_deter`
2. **Tolerance**: Use `< 1e-5` for output comparison, `< 1e-2` for integration test sums
3. **Generator handling**: Check if `scheduler.step()` accepts `generator` parameter
4. **Set timesteps**: Most schedulers require `scheduler.set_timesteps(num_inference_steps)` before use
5. **Device testing**: Use `torch_device` from testing_utils when testing device compatibility
6. **Namespace**: All scheduler test classes end with `Test` (e.g., `DDPMSchedulerTest`)

This comprehensive guide covers the complete testing patterns used across the Hugging Face diffusers library for scheduler testing.

    scheduler_class = self.scheduler_classes[0]
    scheduler_config = self.get_scheduler_config(**config)
    scheduler = scheduler_class(**scheduler_config)
    
    num_inference_steps = self.num_inference_steps
    scheduler.set_timesteps(num_inference_steps)
    timesteps = scheduler.timesteps
    
    # Reset using custom timesteps
    scheduler = scheduler_class(**scheduler_config)
    scheduler.set_timesteps(num_inference_steps=None, timesteps=timesteps)
    
    generator = torch.manual_seed(0)
    model = self.dummy_model()
    sample = self.dummy_sample_deter * scheduler.init_noise_sigma
    
    for i, t in enumerate(scheduler.timesteps):
        sample = scheduler.scale_model_input(sample, t)
        model_output = model(sample, t)
        output = scheduler.step(model_output, t, sample, generator=generator)
        sample = output.prev_sample
    
    return sample

def test_full_loop_custom_timesteps(self):
    sample = self.full_loop_custom_timesteps()
    result_sum = torch.sum(torch.abs(sample))
    assert abs(result_sum.item() - EXPECTED_VALUE) < 1e-2
```

### 8. **Configuration Parameter Tests**

**Standard patterns** (from test_scheduler_ddpm.py):

```python
def test_schedules(self):
    for schedule in ["linear", "squaredcos_cap_v2"]:
        self.check_over_configs(beta_schedule=schedule)

def test_prediction_type(self):
    for prediction_type in ["epsilon", "sample", "v_prediction"]:
        self.check_over_configs(prediction_type=prediction_type)

def test_variance_type(self):
    for variance in ["fixed_small", "fixed_large", "other"]:
        self.check_over_configs(variance_type=variance)

def test_thresholding(self):
    self.check_over_configs(thresholding=False)
    for threshold in [0.5, 1.0, 2.0]:
        for prediction_type in ["epsilon", "sample", "v_prediction"]:
            self.check_over_configs(
                thresholding=True,
                prediction_type=prediction_type,
                sample_max_value=threshold,
            )
```

### 9. **Full Integration Tests**

**Pattern** (custom test methods):

```python
def test_full_loop_no_noise(self):
    scheduler_class = self.scheduler_classes[0]
    scheduler_config = self.get_scheduler_config()
    scheduler = scheduler_class(**scheduler_config)
    
    num_trained_timesteps = len(scheduler)
    
    model = self.dummy_model()
    sample = self.dummy_sample_deter
    generator = torch.manual_seed(0)
    
    for t in reversed(range(num_trained_timesteps)):
        residual = model(sample, t)
        pred_prev_sample = scheduler.step(residual, t, sample, 
                                         generator=generator).prev_sample
        sample = pred_prev_sample
    
    result_sum = torch.sum(torch.abs(sample))
    result_mean = torch.mean(torch.abs(sample))
    
    assert abs(result_sum.item() - 258.9606) < 1e-2
    assert abs(result_mean.item() - 0.3372) < 1e-3

def test_full_loop_with_noise(self):
    # Test with noise injection
    scheduler_class = self.scheduler_classes[0]
    scheduler_config = self.get_scheduler_config()
    scheduler = scheduler_class(**scheduler_config)
    
    num_trained_timesteps = len(scheduler)
    t_start = num_trained_timesteps - 2
    
    model = self.dummy_model()
    sample = self.dummy_sample_deter
    generator = torch.manual_seed(0)
    
    # Add noise
    noise = self.dummy_noise_deter
    timesteps = scheduler.timesteps[t_start * scheduler.order :]
    sample = scheduler.add_noise(sample, noise, timesteps[:1])
    
    for t in timesteps:
        residual = model(sample, t)
        pred_prev_sample = scheduler.step(residual, t, sample, 
                                         generator=generator).prev_sample
        sample = pred_prev_sample
    
    result_sum = torch.sum(torch.abs(sample))
    result_mean = torch.mean(torch.abs(sample))
    
    assert abs(result_sum.item() - 387.9466) < 1e-2
    assert abs(result_mean.item() - 0.5051) < 1e-3
```

### 10. **Skipping Tests**

For schedulers that don't support certain features:

```python
@unittest.skip("Test not supported.")
def test_from_save_pretrained(self):
    pass

@unittest.skip("Test not supported.")
def test_add_noise_device(self):
    pass
```

### 11. **Scheduler Base Class Details**

**File**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775499384049-cb77l1/src/diffusers/schedulers/scheduling_utils.py`

**Key Class**: `SchedulerMixin`

- Provides `from_pretrained()` and `save_pretrained()` methods
- Uses `ConfigMixin` for config management
- Class attribute: `_compatibles` - list of compatible scheduler classes
- Class attribute: `has_compatibles` - boolean flag
- Config file: `scheduler_config.json`

### 12. **File Structure Summary**

```
tests/schedulers/
├── test_schedulers.py          # SchedulerBaseTests, SchedulerCommonTest (868 lines)
├── test_scheduler_ddpm.py      # Simple single-scheduler test
├── test_scheduler_ddim.py      # With forward_default_kwargs
├── test_scheduler_ddpm_parallel.py  # Parallel processing variant
├── test_scheduler_dpm_multi.py # Multi-step solver with state management
├── test_scheduler_deis.py      # DEIS specific tests
├── test_scheduler_ddim_inverse.py   # Inverse variant
├── test_scheduler_euler.py     # With custom timesteps/sigmas
├── test_scheduler_*.py         # ~27 individual scheduler test files
└── __init__.py
```

### 13. **Key Testing Utilities**

From `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775499384049-cb77l1/tests/testing_utils.py`:

- `torch_device` - Device for tests (CPU/GPU/MPS)
- `CaptureLogger` - Capture logging output
- `assert_tensors_close()` - Tensor comparison with tolerance

### 14. **Pattern for Creating New Scheduler Tests**

**Minimal template**:

```python
import torch
from diffusers import MyNewScheduler
from .test_schedulers import SchedulerCommonTest

class MyNewSchedulerTest(SchedulerCommonTest):
    scheduler_classes = (MyNewScheduler,)
    # Optional: specify forward_default_kwargs if scheduler.step() requires specific kwargs
    forward_default_kwargs = ()
    
    def get_scheduler_config(self, **kwargs):
        """Required: return dict with all scheduler __init__ parameters"""
        config = {
            "num_train_timesteps": 1000,
            "beta_start": 0.0001,
            "beta_end": 0.02,
            "beta_schedule": "linear",
            # ... other required config params
        }
        config.update(**kwargs)
        return config
    
    # Add scheduler-specific config parameter tests
    def test_beta_schedule(self):
        for schedule in ["linear", "scaled_linear"]:
            self.check_over_configs(beta_schedule=schedule)
    
    def test_prediction_type(self):
        for prediction_type in ["epsilon", "v_prediction"]:
            self.check_over_configs(prediction_type=prediction_type)
    
    # Optional: Add integration test
    def test_full_loop_no_noise(self):
        scheduler_class = self.scheduler_classes[0]
        scheduler_config = self.get_scheduler_config()
        scheduler = scheduler_class(**scheduler_config)
        
        model = self.dummy_model()
        sample = self.dummy_sample_deter
        
        scheduler.set_timesteps(50)  # or however your scheduler handles steps
        
        for t in scheduler.timesteps:
            residual = model(sample, t)
            sample = scheduler.step(residual, t, sample).prev_sample
        
        # Verify output with known good values
        result_sum = torch.sum(torch.abs(sample))
        assert abs(result_sum.item() - EXPECTED_VALUE) < 1e-2
```

### 15. **Important Conventions**

1. **Deterministic testing**: Use `torch.manual_seed(0)` and `self.dummy_sample_deter`
2. **Tolerance**: Use `< 1e-5` for output comparison, `< 1e-2` for integration test sums
3. **Generator handling**: Check if `scheduler.step()` accepts `generator` parameter
4. **Set timesteps**: Most schedulers require `scheduler.set_timesteps(num_inference_steps)` before use
5. **Device testing**: Use `torch_device` from testing_utils when testing device compatibility
6. **Namespace**: All scheduler test classes end with `Test` (e.g., `DDPMSchedulerTest`)

This comprehensive guide covers the complete testing patterns used across the Hugging Face diffusers library for scheduler testing.

import importlib
import os
from dataclasses import dataclass
from enum import Enum

import torch
from huggingface_hub.utils import validate_hf_hub_args
from typing_extensions import Self

from ..utils import BaseOutput, PushToHubMixin


SCHEDULER_CONFIG_NAME = "scheduler_config.json"


# NOTE: We make this type an enum because it simplifies usage in docs and prevents
# circular imports when used for `_compatibles` within the schedulers module.
# When it's used as a type in pipelines, it really is a Union because the actual
# scheduler instance is passed in.
class KarrasDiffusionSchedulers(Enum):
    DDIMScheduler = 1
    DDPMScheduler = 2
    PNDMScheduler = 3
    LMSDiscreteScheduler = 4
    EulerDiscreteScheduler = 5
    HeunDiscreteScheduler = 6
    EulerAncestralDiscreteScheduler = 7
    DPMSolverMultistepScheduler = 8
    DPMSolverSinglestepScheduler = 9
    KDPM2DiscreteScheduler = 10
    KDPM2AncestralDiscreteScheduler = 11
    DEISMultistepScheduler = 12
    UniPCMultistepScheduler = 13
    DPMSolverSDEScheduler = 14
    EDMEulerScheduler = 15


AysSchedules = {
    "StableDiffusionTimesteps": [999, 850, 736, 645, 545, 455, 343, 233, 124, 24],
    "StableDiffusionSigmas": [14.615, 6.475, 3.861, 2.697, 1.886, 1.396, 0.963, 0.652, 0.399, 0.152, 0.0],
    "StableDiffusionXLTimesteps": [999, 845, 730, 587, 443, 310, 193, 116, 53, 13],
    "StableDiffusionXLSigmas": [14.615, 6.315, 3.771, 2.181, 1.342, 0.862, 0.555, 0.380, 0.234, 0.113, 0.0],
    "StableDiffusionVideoSigmas": [700.00, 54.5, 15.886, 7.977, 4.248, 1.789, 0.981, 0.403, 0.173, 0.034, 0.0],
}


@dataclass
class SchedulerOutput(BaseOutput):
    """
    Base class for the output of a scheduler's `step` function.

    Args:
        prev_sample (`torch.Tensor` of shape `(batch_size, num_channels, height, width)` for images):
            Computed sample `(x_{t-1})` of previous timestep. `prev_sample` should be used as next model input in the
            denoising loop.
    """

    prev_sample: torch.Tensor


class SchedulerMixin(PushToHubMixin):
    """
    Base class for all schedulers.

    [`SchedulerMixin`] contains common functions shared by all schedulers such as general loading and saving
    functionalities.

    [`ConfigMixin`] takes care of storing the configuration attributes (like `num_train_timesteps`) that are passed to
    the scheduler's `__init__` function, and the attributes can be accessed by `scheduler.config.num_train_timesteps`.

    Class attributes:
        - **_compatibles** (`list[str]`) -- A list of scheduler classes that are compatible with the parent scheduler
          class. Use [`~ConfigMixin.from_config`] to load a different compatible scheduler class (should be overridden
          by parent class).
    """

    config_name = SCHEDULER_CONFIG_NAME
    _compatibles = []
    has_compatibles = True

    @classmethod
    @validate_hf_hub_args
    def from_pretrained(
        cls,
        pretrained_model_name_or_path: str | os.PathLike | None = None,
        subfolder: str | None = None,
        return_unused_kwargs=False,
        **kwargs,
    ) -> Self:
        r"""
        Instantiate a scheduler from a pre-defined JSON configuration file in a local directory or Hub repository.

        Parameters:
            pretrained_model_name_or_path (`str` or `os.PathLike`, *optional*):
                Can be either:

                    - A string, the *model id* (for example `google/ddpm-celebahq-256`) of a pretrained model hosted on
                      the Hub.
                    - A path to a *directory* (for example `./my_model_directory`) containing the scheduler
                      configuration saved with [`~SchedulerMixin.save_pretrained`].
            subfolder (`str`, *optional*):
                The subfolder location of a model file within a larger model repository on the Hub or locally.
            return_unused_kwargs (`bool`, *optional*, defaults to `False`):
                Whether kwargs that are not consumed by the Python class should be returned or not.
            cache_dir (`str | os.PathLike`, *optional*):
                Path to a directory where a downloaded pretrained model configuration is cached if the standard cache
                is not used.
            force_download (`bool`, *optional*, defaults to `False`):
                Whether or not to force the (re-)download of the model weights and configuration files, overriding the
                cached versions if they exist.

            proxies (`dict[str, str]`, *optional*):
                A dictionary of proxy servers to use by protocol or endpoint, for example, `{'http': 'foo.bar:3128',
                'http://hostname': 'foo.bar:4012'}`. The proxies are used on each request.
            output_loading_info(`bool`, *optional*, defaults to `False`):
                Whether or not to also return a dictionary containing missing keys, unexpected keys and error messages.
            local_files_only(`bool`, *optional*, defaults to `False`):
                Whether to only load local model weights and configuration files or not. If set to `True`, the model
                won't be downloaded from the Hub.
            token (`str` or *bool*, *optional*):
                The token to use as HTTP bearer authorization for remote files. If `True`, the token generated from
                `diffusers-cli login` (stored in `~/.huggingface`) is used.
            revision (`str`, *optional*, defaults to `"main"`):
                The specific model version to use. It can be a branch name, a tag name, a commit id, or any identifier
                allowed by Git.

        > [!TIP] > To use private or [gated models](https://huggingface.co/docs/hub/models-gated#gated-models), log-in
        with `hf > auth login`. You can also activate the special >
        ["offline-mode"](https://huggingface.co/diffusers/installation.html#offline-mode) to use this method in a >
        firewalled environment.

        """
        config, kwargs, commit_hash = cls.load_config(
            pretrained_model_name_or_path=pretrained_model_name_or_path,
            subfolder=subfolder,
            return_unused_kwargs=True,
            return_commit_hash=True,
            **kwargs,
        )
        return cls.from_config(config, return_unused_kwargs=return_unused_kwargs, **kwargs)

    def save_pretrained(self, save_directory: str | os.PathLike, push_to_hub: bool = False, **kwargs):
        """
        Save a scheduler configuration object to a directory so that it can be reloaded using the
        [`~SchedulerMixin.from_pretrained`] class method.

        Args:
            save_directory (`str` or `os.PathLike`):
                Directory where the configuration JSON file will be saved (will be created if it does not exist).
            push_to_hub (`bool`, *optional*, defaults to `False`):
                Whether or not to push your model to the Hugging Face Hub after saving it. You can specify the
                repository you want to push to with `repo_id` (will default to the name of `save_directory` in your
                namespace).
            kwargs (`dict[str, Any]`, *optional*):
                Additional keyword arguments passed along to the [`~utils.PushToHubMixin.push_to_hub`] method.
        """
        self.save_config(save_directory=save_directory, push_to_hub=push_to_hub, **kwargs)

    @property
    def compatibles(self):
        """
        Returns all schedulers that are compatible with this scheduler

        Returns:
            `list[SchedulerMixin]`: list of compatible schedulers
        """
        return self._get_compatibles()

    @classmethod
    def _get_compatibles(cls):
        compatible_classes_str = list(set([cls.__name__] + cls._compatibles))
        diffusers_library = importlib.import_module(__name__.split(".")[0])
        compatible_classes = [
            getattr(diffusers_library, c) for c in compatible_classes_str if hasattr(diffusers_library, c)
        ]
        return compatible_classes
